zh.title: 我国成功发射天链二号04星
zh.description: 3 月 26 日 23 时 55 分，长征三号乙运载火箭在西昌成功将天链二号04星送入预定轨道；长征系列第 565 次飞行。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 与诺格取消 Cygnus NG-22 货运任务
zh.description: 因加压货运舱在运输中受损，NASA 与诺斯洛普·格鲁曼取消原计划约 2025 年 6 月发射的 NG-22；下一艘 Cygnus（NG-23）不早于 2025 年秋。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Rocket Lab 部署 OroraTech 野火监测卫星
zh.description: Electron 火箭自新西兰玛西亚将 8 颗 OTC-P1 卫星送入约 550 km 轨道，为德国 OroraTech 野火监测星座一期任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 范登堡发射 Starlink 批次
zh.description: 猎鹰 9 号自加利福尼亚范登堡太空军基地执行 Starlink 组网发射，一级海上回收。（媒体直播与报道）
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 公布 SpaceX Crew-11 乘组
zh.description: NASA 公布执行国际空间站轮换的 Crew-11 任务四名航天员名单，计划不早于 2025 年 7 月发射。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Blue Origin 宣布暂停新谢泼德亚轨道旅游飞行
zh.description: 2026 年 1 月，Blue Origin 宣布 New Shepard 亚轨道飞行器将暂停至少 2 年，将资源转向月球能力开发。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA SpaceX Crew-12 载人飞船发射前往国际空间站
zh.description: 2026 年 2 月 13 日，Crew-12 乘组乘猎鹰 9 号与龙飞船自佛罗里达州卡角 SLC-40 升空，前往国际空间站；为商业载人计划第 12 次轮换任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 长征八号甲运载火箭首飞成功
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 13 日，长征八号甲运载火箭自海南商业航天发射场成功首飞，将卫星互联网低轨 20 组卫星送入轨道。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 长征系列运载火箭三小时内两次发射成功
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 13 日，长征八号甲在海南商业发射场、长征二号丁在西昌卫星发射中心相继升空，分别部署卫星互联网低轨组与试验三十号 03、04 星。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 试验三十号卫星03、04星发射成功
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 13 日，长征二号丁运载火箭自西昌卫星发射中心成功发射试验三十号卫星03、04星。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自范登堡发射 Starlink 17-31 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 13 日，猎鹰 9 号自范登堡太空军基地执行 Starlink 17-31 任务，一级助推器 B1071 完成第 32 次飞行后在海上回收。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 我国成功发射遥感五十号02星
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 15 日，长征六号改运载火箭在太原卫星发射中心成功将遥感五十号 02 星送入预定轨道；长征系列第 633 次飞行。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 快舟十一号遥七运载火箭发射成功
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 16 日，快舟十一号遥七运载火箭自酒泉卫星发射中心成功发射，搭载 8 颗卫星。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 神舟二十一号航天员乘组完成第二次出舱活动
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 16 日，神舟二十一号航天员乘组在空间站机械臂和地面科技人员密切协同下完成第二次出舱活动。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自范登堡发射 Starlink 17-24 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 16 日，猎鹰 9 号自范登堡太空军基地执行 Starlink 17-24 任务，一级助推器 B1088 完成第 14 次飞行后在海上回收，SpaceX Starlink 在轨卫星突破 10,000 颗。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自卡纳维拉尔角发射 Starlink 10-46 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 17 日，猎鹰 9 号自卡纳维拉尔角太空军基地执行 Starlink 10-46 任务，一级助推器 B1090 完成第 11 次飞行后在海上回收。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 航天员完成 ISS US EVA 94 太空行走
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 18 日，NASA 航天员 Jessica Meir 和 Chris Williams 完成 ISS US EVA 94 太空行走，主要任务为准备安装 Roll-Out Solar Arrays (IROSA)。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 任务 SLS 火箭转往发射台
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 19 日，NASA 将 SLS 火箭从 Vehicle Assembly Building 转往发射台，准备执行首次载人绕月任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自卡纳维拉尔角发射 Starlink 10-33 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 19 日，猎鹰 9 号自卡纳维拉尔角太空军基地执行 Starlink 10-33 任务，一级助推器 B1077 完成第 27 次飞行后在海上回收。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Rocket Lab 发射 Strix 地球观测雷达卫星
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 20 日，Electron 火箭自新西兰成功发射 Strix 卫星，为日本 Synspective 公司提供地球观测雷达数据。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自范登堡发射 Starlink 17-15 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 20 日，猎鹰 9 号自范登堡太空军基地执行 Starlink 17-15 任务，一级助推器 B1100 完成第 4 次飞行后在海上回收。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自卡纳维拉尔角发射 Starlink 10-62 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 22 日，猎鹰 9 号自卡纳维拉尔角太空军基地执行 Starlink 10-62 任务，一级助推器 B1078 完成第 27 次飞行后在海上回收。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 公布 200 亿美元月球基地计划
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 24 日，NASA 公布未来 7 年约 200 亿美元的月球基地计划，目标建立半永久性月球基地。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 进步 MS 货运飞船与空间站对接补给第 74 远征队
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 22 日进步 94 自拜科努尔发射，24 日对接国际空间站 Poisk 舱；因交会天线未完全展开，对接阶段曾使用 TORU 手动控制。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 商业空间站企业在国会听证会上反击 NASA 新方案：要求'按原计划行事'
zh.description: 正在开发商业空间站以替代国际空间站的企业在国会小组委员会听证会上表示，希望 NASA '坚持原有计划'，不要做出令行业'困惑'的调整。
zh.author: Tianjiangshuo

zh.title: 我国成功发射四维高景二号05、06星
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 26 日 6 时 51 分，长征二号丁运载火箭在太原卫星发射中心成功将四维高景二号 05、06 星送入预定轨道；长征系列第 634 次发射。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 自范登堡发射 Starlink 17-17 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 26 日，猎鹰 9 号自加利福尼亚州范登堡太空军基地执行 Starlink 17-17 任务，一级助推器海上回收。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA公布Artemis II任务航天服
zh.description: NASA公布了Artemis II任务中4名航天员将穿戴的Orion机组生存系统航天服。
zh.author: Monika Luabeya

zh.title: NASA Artemis II 载人绕月任务进入最后一周倒计时
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 27 日，距 NASA Artemis II 发射仅剩一周，各项准备工作进入最终阶段。本次任务将是自阿波罗时代以来人类首次重返月球轨道。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 中欧联合研制的"微笑"卫星完成发射前准备，计划4月9日发射
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 26 日，中科院与欧洲空间局联合研制的太阳风—磁层相互作用全景成像卫星（ SMILE）在库鲁航天发射中心完成所有发射前准备工作，计划于当地时间 4 月 9 日发射升空。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 我国成功发射试验三十三号卫星
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 27 日，我国在酒泉卫星发射中心使用长征二号丙运载火箭/远征一号S上面级，成功将试验三十三号卫星发射升空，卫星顺利进入预定轨道。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 欧洲之力：Artemis II 任务「欧洲服务舱」详解视频发布
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 27 日，ESA 发布视频详解 Artemis II 任务核心——欧洲服务舱（European Service Module），阐释欧洲如何为人类重返月球提供推进、电力与生命保障。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 欧洲卫星导航增强系统首星即将发射——Rocket Lab 为 ESA 执行 Celeste 任务
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 27 日，ESA 宣布其首批 Celeste 卫星将于 3 月 28 日由 Rocket Lab Electron 火箭从新西兰发射，这是欧洲首个低轨卫星导航增强（LEO-PNT）任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA正在开发首个核动力星际飞船
zh.description: NASA正在开发首个核动力星际飞船，标志着深空探索能力进入新时代。
zh.author: Keith Cooper

zh.title: NASA Artemis II 航天员乘组抵达肯尼迪航天中心
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 27 日，NASA Artemis II 四大航天员乘组及备份乘组抵达肯尼迪航天中心，执行历史性的绕月飞行任务，发射窗口暂定 4 月 1 日。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: ESA Celeste 卫星成功发射——欧洲低轨导航增强时代开启
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 28 日，ESA 首两颗 Celeste 卫星由 Rocket Lab Electron 火箭成功发射升空，欧洲低轨卫星导航增强（LEO-PNT）时代正式开启。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 航天员 Mike Fincke 披露 Crew-11 提前返航医学事件详情
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 28 日，NASA 航天员 Mike Fincke 首次披露了导致 Crew-11 任务提前一个多月返回地球的医学事件详情，表示事发前毫无预兆，持续约 20 分钟后自行缓解，但医生至今未查明原因。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 乘组发射前接受媒体提问，三天后启程赴月
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 29 日，Artemis II 乘组在肯尼迪航天中心隔离设施中远程接受媒体提问，NASA 同步召开新闻发布会介绍任务状态，计划 4 月 1 日发射。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 绕月任务进入最终准备阶段，发射日天气预报 80% 适宜发射
zh.description: NASA 肯尼迪航天中心团队正在进行 Artemis II 发射倒计时的最终准备工作，4 月 1 日发射当天天气预报显示 80% 适宜发射。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 力箭二号首飞成功：中国首款 CBC 构型火箭入轨，商业航天进入大运力时代
zh.description: 中科宇航力箭二号遥一火箭成功首飞，一箭三星入轨。采用通用助推器核心构型，起飞重量625吨，将支撑低轨星座组网与低成本货运。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 将于 3 月 30 日执行 Transporter-16 拼车任务，从范登堡发射 119 个有效载荷
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月 30 日，SpaceX 猎鹰 9 号火箭将从加利福尼亚州范登堡太空军基地执行 Transporter-16 拼车任务，携带 119 个有效载荷进入轨道。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 完成新一代 V3 版星舰静态点火测试，计划 4 月发射
zh.description: 2026 年 3 月，SpaceX 完成了新一代 V3 版星舰的首次静态点火测试，为 4 月的发射任务做准备。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 发射倒计时正式启动，距历史性绕月任务仅剩 2 天
zh.description: NASA Artemis II 发射倒计时已在肯尼迪航天中心正式启动，倒计时时钟于美东时间 16:44 开始计时，目标发射时间为 4 月 1 日 18:24，这是 50 多年来首次载人月球任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: X1.4 级太阳耀斑对 Artemis II 发射无威胁，NASA 确认按计划进行
zh.description: 太阳在 3 月 29 日爆发 X1.4 级强烈耀斑，引发亚洲和澳大利亚无线电中断，但 NASA 确认不会影响 4 月 1 日 Artemis II 载人绕月任务发射。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 国际空间站第 74 远征队乘组备战 Cygnus 货运任务并维护太空行走宇航服
zh.description: 第 74 远征队宇航员为诺斯罗普·格鲁曼 Cygnus XL 货运飞船的到来进行训练，维护上周太空行走使用的宇航服，并在国际空间站上开展紧急医疗演习。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Rocket Lab 获监管批准收购 Mynaric，激光通信技术将纳入内部体系
zh.description: Rocket Lab 获得德国联邦经济事务与能源部监管批准，将收购激光光通信终端领先供应商 Mynaric AG，预计 2026 年 4 月完成交易，进一步强化卫星星座通信能力。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 成功发射：人类 50 多年来首次载人绕月任务启航
zh.description: NASA Artemis II 于 2026 年 4 月 1 日 18:35 EDT 从肯尼迪航天中心 39B 发射台成功升空，4 名航天员开启约 10 天的绕月飞行，这是自 1972 年阿波罗 17 号以来人类首次飞向月球。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 中国初创公司 Sustain Space 在轨验证柔性机械臂，推进在轨服务能力
zh.description: 中国商业航天公司 Sustain Space 的 Xiyuan-0 卫星成功完成柔性机械臂在轨演示，验证了自主加注模拟、遥操作、视觉伺服和力控操作四种模式，标志着中国在商业在轨服务领域取得重要进展。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 飞行第 2 天：TLI 燃烧成功完成，猎户座正式踏上奔月之旅
zh.description: NASA Artemis II 猎户座飞船于美东时间 19:49 成功完成地月转移注入燃烧，点火 5 分 50 秒，4 名航天员正式脱离地球轨道飞向月球。月球科学团队已开始制定飞越观测计划。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 联盟-5（额尔齐斯）运载火箭执行首飞测试：俄罗斯新一代中型火箭从拜科努尔发射
zh.description: 俄罗斯与哈萨克斯坦联合研制的新一代中型运载火箭联盟-5（额尔齐斯）于 4 月 3 日在拜科努尔航天发射场执行首次飞行测试，发射结果待官方确认。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 向 SEC 提交 IPO 申请，有望创下史上最大规模上市纪录
zh.description: 马斯克旗下 SpaceX 已向美国证券交易委员会保密提交 IPO 注册文件，目标估值超过 1.75 万亿美元，融资规模最高可达 500 亿美元，有望超越沙特阿美成为史上最大 IPO。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 天龙三号首飞失利：中国商业航天大运力火箭首次飞行遭遇异常
zh.description: 天兵科技天龙三号运载火箭于北京时间 4 月 3 日 12:17 从酒泉点火升空后飞行异常，首飞任务失利，具体原因正在排查。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 飞行第三日：乘组已过半程奔赴月球，Christina Koch 从对接舱口目视月球
zh.description: Artemis II 任务已过半程，轨道修正取消，Christina Koch 从对接舱口看到月球，乘组正在为 4 月 6 日月球飞越做准备。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Atlas V 成功发射 Amazon Leo 第五批 29 颗卫星，柯伊伯星座加速组网
zh.description: ULA Atlas V 551 从卡纳维拉尔角成功发射 Amazon Leo LA-05 任务，将 29 颗柯伊伯星座卫星送入低地球轨道。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 诺格 CRS-24 货运补给任务定于 4 月 8 日发射，将向 ISS 运送约 5000 公斤物资
zh.description: NASA、诺斯罗普·格鲁曼与 SpaceX 联合执行 CRS-24 商业补给任务，Cygnus XL 飞船将搭载约 11000 磅科研设备与物资飞往国际空间站。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 神舟二十一号乘组完成第二次出舱，即将达成在轨 150 天里程碑
zh.description: 张陆、武飞、张洪章三名航天员圆满完成第二次出舱活动，开展药代动力学实验、燃烧科学实验等系列任务，在轨即将满 150 天。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 中欧联合「微笑」卫星完成发射前准备，4 月 9 日乘织女星-C 火箭升空
zh.description: 中国科学院与 ESA 联合研制的 SMILE 卫星已完成库鲁航天中心全部发射前准备，定于 4 月 9 日由 Vega-C 火箭发射升空，将首次实现地球磁层大尺度整体成像。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 飞行第四日：深空飞行与月球飞越准备，Glover 将手动操控 Orion
zh.description: Artemis II 第四飞行日，乘组距地球约 16.9 万英里，Victor Glover 将手动操控 Orion 测试深空飞行性能，为 4 月 6 日月球飞越做最后准备。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 奔月途中遭遇 Orion 卫生间故障：废水管结冰与烧焦气味
zh.description: 猎户座飞船在飞行第三日出现卫生间废水排放口结冰及烧焦气味问题，乘组暂时使用应急集尿袋，地面控制中心正制定除冰方案。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 嫦娥四号数据发现地月空间"宇宙线空腔"，为深空辐射防护提供新思路
zh.description: 山东大学团队利用嫦娥四号三年观测数据，首次在月球轨道发现银河宇宙线通量显著降低的空间区域，突破传统认知。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 我国首次精准测定月壤单颗粒热导率，发现天然超绝热材料
zh.description: 中科院联合团队首次实现嫦娥五号月壤单颗粒热导率精准测量，发现胶结物颗粒热导率低至约8 mW·m⁻¹·K⁻¹，媲美人造气凝胶。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 沙特Shams卫星随Artemis II升空，开启首次国家级空间天气监测任务
zh.description: 沙特沙姆斯卫星随Artemis II发射升空并建立通信,将在高椭圆轨道监测太阳活动与空间辐射
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 神舟二十一号航天员在轨工作超五个月 空间站多项科学实验稳步推进
zh.description: 神舟二十一号航天员乘组张陆、武飞、张洪章已在轨驻留超五个月，完成多项航天医学实验与站务管理任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: SpaceX 星舰 V3 首飞再推迟至五月，已是第三次延期
zh.description: 马斯克宣布星舰 V3 首飞推迟 4-6 周，从四月延至五月初，这已是第三次延期。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 蓝箭航天张晓东：朱雀三号上半年冲刺回收复用，打造太空算力低成本「班车」
zh.description: 蓝箭航天朱雀三号总设计师张晓东在2026太空算力产业大会上表示，2026年上半年遥二箭将再次开展回收试验，力争第四季度实现首次回收复用飞行。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 2026年中国航天大会（CSC2026）将于4月23-26日在成都市举办
zh.description: 2026年中国航天大会以“七秩问天路 携手探九霄”为主题，将于2026年4月23-26日在成都市举办，广邀国内外航天领域知名专家、学者、管理者，共商航天高质量发展蓝图。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 月球飞越完成：打破 Apollo 13 最远载人航天记录，距地球 406,771 公里
zh.description: Artemis II 乘组成功完成月球飞越，Orion 飞船以距月球表面约 6,500 公里的高度掠过月球背面，超越 Apollo 13 创下的载人航天最远距离记录，达 406,771 公里。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 匈牙利选定诺格公司建造首颗国家地球静止轨道通信卫星 HUSAT
zh.description: 匈牙利与诺斯罗普·格鲁曼公司签署合同，建造其首颗国家地球静止轨道通信卫星 HUSAT，由匈牙利 4iG 集团和诺格联合开发，预计 2028 年发射。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 局长 Isaacson 为大幅削减预算方案辩护，称将聚焦'大胆目标'
zh.description: NASA 局长 Jared Isaacman 在国会听证会上为特朗普政府提出的 NASA 预算削减方案辩护，该方案提议将 NASA 预算削减约 20%，科学项目首当其冲。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA 暂停 SLS 移动发射台 2（ML-2）建造工作，Artemis IV 及后续任务面临不确定性
zh.description: NASA 已通知承包商 Bechtel 停止 SLS Block 1B 移动发射台 2（ML-2）的建造工作，该项目已累计投入超过 10 亿美元但严重超支超期，影响 Artemis IV 及后续任务时间表。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Starfish Space 获超 1 亿美元融资，推进在轨服务航天器 Otter
zh.description: 在轨服务初创公司 Starfish Space 完成超过 1 亿美元的新一轮融资，资金将用于推进其 Otter 在轨服务航天器的开发和首次任务。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 诺格 CRS-24 国际空间站货运任务再度延期，发射日期推迟至 4 月 11 日
zh.description: Northrop Grumman 第 24 次商业补给服务任务（NG-24）发射日期从原定的 4 月 8 日三度推迟至 4 月 11 日，Cygnus 货运飞船将携带超过 8,000 磅物资飞往国际空间站。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 德国 Isar Aerospace Spectrum 火箭即将执行第二次试飞，欧洲商业发射迎来关键考验
zh.description: 德国商业火箭公司 Isar Aerospace 计划于 4 月 9 日从挪威安岛航天中心发射 Spectrum 火箭执行第二次试飞，搭载 5 颗立方星和 1 个技术验证载荷，此前因天气和技术原因已三度推迟。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 中欧联合 SMILE 卫星发射推迟：Vega-C 火箭子系统技术问题，新日期待定
zh.description: 欧空局（ESA）宣布原定于 4 月 9 日由 Vega-C 火箭发射的中欧联合 SMILE 科学卫星因技术问题推迟，新发射日期待定。SMILE 由 ESA 和中国科学院联合研制，用于研究太阳风与地球磁层的相互作用。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: Artemis II 科学实验全面展开，猎户座飞船开始返航地球
zh.description: Artemis II 机组完成月球飞越后采集超 175 GB 图像数据，实验性激光通信载荷在 45 分钟内传回 20 GB，溅落定于北京时间 4 月 11 日上午。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 嫦娥七号探测器全部运抵文昌发射场，2026年下半年择机发射
zh.description: 嫦娥七号探测器已安全运抵文昌航天发射场，将突破高精度月面软着陆等关键技术，对月球南极区域开展综合探测
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 长征火箭连续发射：千帆星座新增 18 星、国网星座第 21 批入轨
zh.description: 中国本周分别使用长征 8 号和长征 6A 火箭为千帆和国网巨型星座发射卫星，千帆在轨卫星达 126 颗，国网运行阶段卫星达 168 颗。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 欧洲航天局Celeste卫星成功广播首个导航信号，开启低轨导航新时代
zh.description: ESA的Celeste卫星成功从低地球轨道广播首个导航信号，标志着欧洲低轨导航系统建设的重要里程碑。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 长征六号甲首发巨型星座卫星，低轨21组卫星成功入轨
zh.description: 北京时间2026年4月9日03:38，长征六号甲遥十七运载火箭成功将卫星互联网低轨21组卫星送入预定极地轨道。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: NASA暂停「月球门户」空间站计划，转向建设月球表面基地
zh.description: NASA宣布暂停原定的「月球门户」(Lunar Gateway)空间站计划，投入200亿美元在7年内分阶段建设月球南极附近永久表面基地，战略从「在月球轨道上修桥铺路」转变为「直接在月面建设基地」。
zh.author: 天疆说

zh.title: 按日期查阅 · Space News
zh.description: 按年月分组的全部航天新闻条目索引。
zh.author: 天疆说

en.title: China launches Tianlian II-04 data-relay satellite
en.description: Long March 3B lifts off from Xichang on March 26, 2025, placing the Tianlian II-04 GEO relay satellite into planned orbit; 565th Long March family flight.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA and Northrop cancel Cygnus NG-22 cargo mission
en.description: Pressurized cargo module damage in transit leads NASA and Northrop Grumman to drop the planned June Cygnus NG-22 ISS flight; NG-23 targeted no earlier than fall 2025.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Rocket Lab launches eight OroraTech wildfire satellites
en.description: Electron deploys eight OTC-P1 satellites for Germany’s OroraTech from New Zealand; Phase 1 of a planned larger constellation for wildfire monitoring.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink launch from Vandenberg
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts Starlink satellites from Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, with booster landing at sea (press coverage).
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA announces SpaceX Crew-11 crew assignments
en.description: NASA names four crew members for the next SpaceX Crew Dragon rotation to the ISS, targeting launch no earlier than July 2025 (RELEASE 25-010).
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Blue Origin pauses New Shepard suborbital tourism flights
en.description: Blue Origin announces New Shepard suborbital flights to pause for at least 2 years, focusing on lunar capabilities development.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA’s SpaceX Crew-12 launches to the International Space Station
en.description: On Feb. 13, 2026, Crew-12 lifted off on a Falcon 9 and Dragon from Cape Canaveral SLC-40 for the 12th Commercial Crew rotation mission to the ISS.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Long March 8A maiden flight success
en.description: Long March 8A rocket makes successful maiden flight from Hainan Commercial Space Launch Site on March 13, 2026, delivering 20 satellite internet LEO satellites.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Two Long March launches within three hours in China
en.description: On Mar. 13, 2026, Long March-8A lifted off from Hainan and Long March-2D from Xichang, deploying a LEO satellite internet batch and Shiyan 30C/30D satellites.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Shiyan-30 03 & 04 satellites launched
en.description: Long March 2D rocket launches Shiyan-30 03 & 04 satellites from Xichang on March 13, 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 17-31 launch from Vandenberg
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 25 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites from Vandenberg SFB on March 13, 2026; booster B1071 lands on OCISLY.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: China launches Yaogan-50 02 remote sensing satellite
en.description: On Mar. 15, 2026, a modified Long March-6 lifted Yaogan-50 02 from Taiyuan; the flight was the 633rd mission of the Long March series.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Kuaizhou-11 Yaoyi-7 rocket launches with 8 satellites
en.description: Kuaizhou-11 Yaoyi-7 rocket launches from Jiuquan on March 16, 2026, carrying 8 satellites including Juntian-1 04A and others.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Shenzhou-21 crew completes second EVA
en.description: Shenzhou-21 crew members Zhang Lu, Wu Fei, and Zhang Hongzhang complete second extravehicular activity on March 16, 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 17-24 launch from Vandenberg
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 25 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg SFB on March 16, 2026; booster B1088 lands on OCISLY; SpaceX Starlink reaches 10,000 satellites in orbit.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 10-46 launch from Cape Canaveral
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 29 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites from Cape Canaveral SFS on March 17, 2026; booster B1090 lands on ASOG.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA astronauts complete ISS US EVA 94
en.description: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Chris Williams complete US EVA 94 outside the ISS on March 18, 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II SLS rocket rolls out to launch pad
en.description: NASA rolls SLS rocket from VAB to Pad 39B on March 19, 2026, ahead of first crewed lunar orbit mission planned for April.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 10-33 launch from Cape Canaveral
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 29 Starlink V2 Mini satellites from Cape Canaveral SFS on March 19, 2026; booster B1077 lands on JRTI.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Rocket Lab launches Strix Earth observation radar satellite
en.description: Electron rocket launches Strix satellite for Japan's Synspective from New Zealand on March 20, 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 17-15 launch from Vandenberg
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 25 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites from Vandenberg SFB on March 20, 2026; booster B1100 lands on OCISLY.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 10-62 launch from Cape Canaveral
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 29 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites from Cape Canaveral SFS on March 22, 2026; booster B1078 lands on ASOG.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA unveils $20 billion lunar base plan
en.description: NASA announces $20 billion plan over seven years to establish semi-permanent lunar base on March 24, 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Progress 94 cargo ship docks to the ISS for Expedition 74
en.description: Progress 94 launched from Baikonur on Mar. 22, 2026, and docked to the Poisk module on Mar. 24; TORU manual control was used after a KURS antenna failed to deploy.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Commercial Space Station Companies Push Back on NASA's New Plan at House Hearing
en.description: Companies developing commercial space stations to replace the ISS told a House subcommittee they want NASA to 'stick with the plan' and not make changes that are 'sowing confusion' in the industry.
en.author: SpacePolicyOnline

en.title: China launches Siwei Gaojing-2 05 & 06 satellites
en.description: Long March 2D lifts off from Taiyuan on March 26, 2026, placing two commercial radar Earth-observation satellites into orbit; 634th Long March family flight.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starlink 17-17 launch from Vandenberg
en.description: Falcon 9 lifts 25 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg SFB on March 26, 2026; booster B1081 lands on OCISLY.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA Unveils Artemis II Crew's Survival Suits
en.description: NASA reveals the Orion Crew Survival System suits that the four Artemis II astronauts will wear for the first crewed lunar flight since Apollo.
en.author: Monika Luabeya

en.title: NASA Artemis II Crewed Lunar Mission Enters Final Week Countdown
en.description: On March 27, 2026, NASA Artemis II is one week from launch — the first crewed lunar orbit flight since Apollo 17 in 1972.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: China-Europe SMILE Satellite Completes Pre-Launch Preparations Ahead of April 9 Launch
en.description: The China Academy of Sciences announced on March 26, 2026 that the China-ESA joint SMILE satellite has completed all pre-launch preparations at the Kourou Launch Center, scheduled for April 9 local time aboard Vega-C.
en.author: CNSA / ESA

en.title: China Launches Shiyan-33 Satellite for Space Environment Testing
en.description: China successfully launched the Shiyan-33 satellite on March 27, 2026, using a Long March 2C rocket with Yuanzheng-1S upper stage from Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center.
en.author: CNSA

en.title: How Europe Will Power the Journey to the Moon and Back - ESA Artemis II Service Module Video
en.description: On March 27, 2026, ESA released a video explaining the European Service Module for Artemis II - providing propulsion, power, and life support for the crewed lunar orbit mission.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: ESA's First Celeste Satellites Set for Launch Aboard Rocket Lab Electron
en.description: On March 27, 2026, ESA announced the first two Celeste satellites will launch on March 28 at 10:14 CET aboard a Rocket Lab Electron rocket from New Zealand — Europe's first LEO-PNT demonstration mission.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA Developing First Nuclear-Powered Interplanetary Spacecraft
en.description: NASA is working on the first nuclear-powered interplanetary spacecraft, marking a new era in deep space exploration capabilities.
en.author: Keith Cooper

en.title: Artemis II Crew Arrives at Kennedy Space Center Ahead of Moon Mission
en.description: The four Artemis II astronauts and their backups arrived at Kennedy Space Center on March 27, 2026, ahead of their historic flight around the Moon scheduled for April 1.
en.author: SpacePolicyOnline

en.title: ESA's First Celeste Satellites Successfully Launched — Europe's LEO Navigation Augmentation Era Begins
en.description: On March 28, 2026, ESA's first two Celeste satellites were successfully launched aboard Rocket Lab's Electron rocket from New Zealand, marking the beginning of Europe's LEO-PNT era.
en.author: ESA

en.title: NASA Astronaut Mike Fincke Shares Details About Medical Event That Ended Crew-11 Mission Early
en.description: NASA astronaut Mike Fincke has revealed more about the medical event that caused the Crew-11 mission to return to Earth more than a month ahead of schedule in January 2026.
en.author: SpacePolicyOnline

en.title: Artemis II Crew Answers Media Questions as Launch Week Begins
en.description: On March 29, 2026, the Artemis II crew answered reporters' questions from quarantine while NASA officials provided a mission status update, three days before the planned April 1 launch.
en.author: SpacePolicyOnline

en.title: Final Preparations Underway for Artemis II Moon Mission, 80% Go Weather Forecast
en.description: NASA teams at Kennedy Space Center are making final preparations for the Artemis II launch countdown, with an 80% chance of favorable weather for the April 1 target.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Lift-2 Maiden Flight Success: China's First CBC-Configuration Rocket Reaches Orbit
en.description: CAS Space's Lift-2 rocket completed its maiden flight on March 30, 2026, deploying three satellites. The 625-ton vehicle is China's first Common Booster Core configuration launch vehicle.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Set to Launch 119 Payloads on Transporter-16 Rideshare Mission From Vandenberg
en.description: On March 30, 2026, SpaceX's Falcon 9 will launch the Transporter-16 rideshare mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base, carrying 119 payloads to orbit.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Fires Up Next-Gen V3 Starship for First Time Ahead of April Launch
en.description: SpaceX completed the first static fire test of its next-generation V3 Starship in March 2026, preparing for an April launch attempt.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Launch Countdown Officially Underway, T-2 Days to Historic Moon Mission
en.description: NASA's Artemis II launch countdown has begun at Kennedy Space Center, with the clock ticking toward an April 1 target launch time of 6:24 p.m. EDT for the first crewed Moon mission in over 50 years.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: X1.4 Solar Flare Poses No Threat to Artemis II Launch, NASA Confirms
en.description: A massive X1.4-class solar flare erupted from the Sun on March 29, triggering radio blackouts over Asia and Australia, but NASA says space weather will not affect the Artemis II crewed Moon launch on April 1.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: ISS Expedition 74 Crew Kicks Off Week With Cygnus Cargo Training and Spacewalk Cleanup
en.description: Expedition 74 astronauts trained for the upcoming Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL cargo mission, serviced spacesuits from last week's EVA, and conducted emergency medical drills aboard the International Space Station.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Rocket Lab Receives Regulatory Approval to Acquire Mynaric, Bringing Laser Communications In-House
en.description: Rocket Lab has received German regulatory approval to acquire Mynaric AG, a leading provider of laser optical communications terminals. The deal is expected to close in April 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Successfully Launches: First Crewed Lunar Mission in Over 50 Years
en.description: NASA's Artemis II launched on April 1, 2026, at 6:35 PM EDT from Kennedy Space Center's Launch Pad 39B, sending four astronauts on an approximately 10-day journey around the Moon — the first crewed lunar flight since Apollo 17 in 1972.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Chinese Startup Sustain Space Validates Flexible Robotic Arm On-Orbit, Advancing Servicing Capabilities
en.description: Sustain Space's Xiyuan-0 satellite successfully completed on-orbit demonstrations of a flexible robotic arm, verifying four operation modes including autonomous refueling simulation, teleoperation, and force-controlled manipulation.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Flight Day 2: TLI Burn Complete, Orion Officially En Route to the Moon
en.description: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully completed the translunar injection burn at 7:49 PM EDT, firing for 5 minutes and 50 seconds. The four astronauts are now on their way to the Moon for the first time in 53 years.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Soyuz-5 (Irtysh) Medium-Launch Vehicle Flight Test from Baikonur
en.description: Russia's next-generation medium-class launch vehicle Soyuz-5, also known as Irtysh, conducted its first test flight from Baikonur Cosmodrome on April 3. Launch result pending official confirmation.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Files Confidential IPO Registration with SEC, Targeting Record-Breaking Public Offering
en.description: SpaceX has confidentially filed for an IPO with the SEC, aiming for a valuation exceeding $1.75 trillion and potentially raising up to $50 billion — which would surpass Saudi Aramco as the largest IPO in history.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Tianlong-3 Maiden Flight Fails: China's Falcon 9-Class Commercial Rocket Loses Control After Liftoff
en.description: Space Pioneer's Tianlong-3 rocket launched from Jiuquan on April 3 but experienced an anomaly ~33 seconds after liftoff, resulting in loss of the vehicle. The company has initiated a failure investigation.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Flight Day 3: Crew More Than Halfway to the Moon, Koch Sights Moon from Docking Hatch
en.description: Artemis II is more than halfway to the Moon. The trajectory correction burn was cancelled, Christina Koch spotted the Moon from the docking hatch, and the crew is preparing for the April 6 lunar flyby.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Atlas V Launches 29 Amazon Leo Satellites on LA-05 Mission, Accelerating Constellation Buildout
en.description: ULA Atlas V 551 successfully launched 29 Amazon Leo broadband internet satellites from Cape Canaveral SFS on the LA-05 mission.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Resupply Mission Targeting April 8 Launch to ISS
en.description: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 launch, delivering approximately 11,000 pounds of science and cargo to the International Space Station.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Shenzhou-21 Crew Completes Second EVA, Approaching 150-Day On-Orbit Milestone
en.description: Taikonauts Zhang Lu, Wu Fei, and Zhang Hongzhang have completed their second spacewalk and continue science experiments aboard the Tiangong space station, nearing 150 days in orbit.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: China-ESA SMILE Satellite Completes Launch Preparations, Set for April 9 Vega-C Liftoff
en.description: The SMILE satellite, jointly developed by CAS and ESA, has completed all launch preparations at Kourou and is scheduled for launch on April 9 aboard a Vega-C rocket.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Flight Day 4: Deep-Space Flying, Lunar Flyby Prep as Glover Takes Manual Control
en.description: On Flight Day 4, the Artemis II crew is approximately 169,000 miles from Earth. Victor Glover will manually pilot Orion to test deep-space handling qualities, and the crew is preparing for Monday's lunar flyby.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Orion Spacecraft Toilet Malfunction: Frozen Waste Line and Burning Smell En Route to Moon
en.description: Orion spacecraft experienced a burning odor and frozen waste vent on Flight Day 3 of Artemis II. Crew temporarily using emergency urine bags while ground control works on a de-icing plan.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Chang'e-4 Data Reveals 'Cosmic Ray Cavity' in Cislunar Space
en.description: A Shandong University team discovers a significant reduction in galactic cosmic ray flux near lunar orbit using three years of Chang'e-4 data, offering new insights for deep space radiation protection.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: China Achieves First Precise Thermal Conductivity Measurement of Single Lunar Soil Particles
en.description: A joint research team measured Chang'e-5 lunar soil single-particle thermal conductivity, discovering agglutinate particles with conductivity as low as ~8 mW·m⁻¹·K⁻¹, rivaling synthetic aerogels.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Saudi Arabia's 'Shams' Satellite Launches Aboard Artemis II on First National Space Weather Mission
en.description: The Saudi-developed Shams satellite has established initial communication after launching on Artemis II, marking the kingdom's first national-level space weather monitoring mission.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Shenzhou-21 Crew Surpasses Five Months in Orbit, Space Station Experiments Progress
en.description: Shenzhou-21 crew members Zhang Lu, Wu Fei, and Zhang Hongzhang have been in orbit for over five months, completing multiple space medicine experiments and station maintenance tasks.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: SpaceX Starship V3 First Flight Delayed to May — Third Consecutive Postponement
en.description: Elon Musk announced Starship V3's maiden flight is delayed another 4-6 weeks, pushing from April to early May in the third delay this year.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: LandSpace's Zhang Xiaodong: Zhuque-3 to Sprint for Reuse Testing in First Half of 2026, Building Low-Cost 'Bus' for Space Computing
en.description: At the 2026 Space Computing Industry Conference, Zhang Xiaodong, chief designer of LandSpace's reusable Zhuque-3 rocket, announced plans for recovery tests in H1 2026 and aiming for the first reusable flight by Q4 2026.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: 2026 China Space Conference (CSC2026) to be Held in Chengdu on April 23-26
en.description: The 2026 China Space Conference (CSC2026), themed 'Seventy Years Exploring the Heavens, Journeying the Nine Realms Together,' will be held in Chengdu from April 23-26, 2026, inviting renowned experts, scholars, and managers from domestic and international aerospace fields to discuss the blueprint for high-quality aerospace development.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Completes Lunar Flyby: Breaks Apollo 13 Distance Record at 406,771 km from Earth
en.description: Artemis II crew successfully completed the lunar flyby, with Orion passing about 6,500 km above the Moon's far side and surpassing Apollo 13's crewed distance record at 406,771 km from Earth.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Hungary Taps Northrop Grumman for First National GEO Communications Satellite HUSAT
en.description: Hungary signed a contract with Northrop Grumman to build HUSAT, its first national geostationary communications satellite, developed jointly with Hungary's 4iG Group and targeting a 2028 launch.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA Administrator Isaacman Defends Steep Budget Cuts, Pledges Focus on 'Bold Goals'
en.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman defended the Trump administration's proposed ~20% budget cut to NASA during congressional hearings, arguing the agency will be more efficient and focus on bold exploration goals.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA Halts Work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2, Casting Doubt on Artemis IV and Beyond
en.description: NASA has ordered a stop-work on Mobile Launcher 2 (ML-2) built by Bechtel, with the project exceeding $1 billion in spending amid massive cost overruns and delays, threatening the Artemis IV timeline.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Starfish Space Raises Over $100 Million to Advance Otter On-Orbit Servicing Vehicle
en.description: In-orbit servicing startup Starfish Space closed a new funding round exceeding $100 million, aimed at accelerating development of its Otter servicing spacecraft and its first GEO mission.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Northrop Grumman CRS-24 ISS Resupply Mission Delayed Again to April 11
en.description: NG-24 launch has been postponed three times from its original April 8 date to NET April 11, 7:41 a.m. EDT. The Cygnus cargo spacecraft will carry over 8,000 lbs of science and supplies to the ISS.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Isar Aerospace Spectrum Rocket Set for Second Test Flight, a Key Test for European Commercial Launch
en.description: German launch startup Isar Aerospace plans to launch its Spectrum rocket on a second test flight from Norway's Andøya Spaceport on April 9, carrying 5 CubeSats and 1 technology payload, after three prior delays.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: China-ESA Joint SMILE Satellite Launch Postponed Due to Vega-C Technical Issue, New Date TBC
en.description: ESA announced the postponement of the SMILE science satellite launch, originally scheduled for April 9 on a Vega-C rocket, due to a technical issue with a subsystem component. A new launch date is yet to be determined.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Artemis II Science Underway as Orion Begins Return Trip to Earth
en.description: Artemis II crew captured over 175 GB of images during lunar flyby; experimental laser comms payload returned 20 GB in 45 minutes. Splashdown set for April 10, 8:07 PM EDT off San Diego.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Chang'e 7 Probe Arrives at Wenchang Launch Center for Planned 2026 Launch
en.description: Chang'e 7 probe has safely arrived at Wenchang launch center, targeting breakthrough technologies for lunar south pole exploration
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: China Launches Long March Rockets for Thousand Sails and Guowang Megaconstellations
en.description: China conducted two Long March launches this week, adding 18 satellites to the Thousand Sails constellation (126 total) and the 21st group to Guowang (168 operational satellites).
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: ESA's Celeste Satellite Successfully Broadcasts First Navigation Signal, Launching New LEO Navigation Era
en.description: ESA's Celeste satellite successfully broadcasts first navigation signal from low Earth orbit, marking a significant milestone for Europe's low-orbit navigation system.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Long March-6A Launches Mega Constellation Satellites, Successfully Deploys 21 Low-Earth Orbit Satellites
en.description: At 03:38 Beijing Time on April 9, 2026, Long March-6A YA-17 rocket successfully deployed 21 low-Earth orbit satellite internet satellites into the intended polar orbit.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: NASA Suspends Lunar Gateway Space Station Plan, Shifts to Building Lunar Surface Base
en.description: NASA announced the suspension of the planned Lunar Gateway space station, investing $20 billion over 7 years to build a permanent lunar surface base near the South Pole, shifting strategy from 'building bridges in lunar orbit' to 'directly establishing a base on the lunar surface'.
en.author: Tianjiangshuo

en.title: Archive · Space News
en.description: All Space News posts grouped by year and month.
en.author: 天疆说
